pricing.source: OpenAI API pricing, standard short-context rates, 2026-06-24

hardChecks.label: Citation source ids
hardChecks.expectation: Every cited source id exists in the source packet

hardChecks.label: Cost metadata
hardChecks.expectation: estimatedUsd is present when pricing is configured

hardChecks.label: Token metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Input, cached-input, and output token counts are coherent

hardChecks.label: Provider and model metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Provider, model, and prompt version are present

hardChecks.label: Judge input boundary
hardChecks.expectation: Judge prompt omits eval-only object keys and holdout tuning fields

hardChecks.label: Judge claim coverage
hardChecks.expectation: LLM judge returns exactly one judgment for every briefing claim

claimJudgments.claimText: Quickstart completion increased from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, and exits decreased at the first API-call step, but the largest remaining drop occurs during local setup and credential configuration due to environment-specific errors.
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 directly states the 44% to 58% increase after the copy refresh, fewer exits at the first API call step, and the largest remaining drop during local setup and credential configuration from environment-specific errors.

claimJudgments.claimText: Completion gains were strongest for users who already had a valid token; new users without a configured environment still hit the same setup wall, and the team cautioned against treating the metric as end-to-end onboarding success until credential setup has a recovery path.
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 explicitly supports all parts: gains were strongest for users with valid tokens, new users without configured environments still hit the setup wall, and the success metric should not be presented as an end-to-end onboarding win until credential setup has recovery.

claimJudgments.claimText: Support tickets from new users most often involve environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users frequently lack clear recovery steps after failed installs.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 says most new tickets mention environment drift, stale CLI versions, and unclear recovery after failed installs, and groups tickets into stale CLI installs, missing environment variables, and wrong-workspace credentials.

claimJudgments.claimText: An internal support checklist resolves most setup cases, but key steps are not in public docs, and tickets sometimes reopen when users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same broken state.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 states the internal checklist resolved most cases, relied on steps missing from public docs, and several tickets reopened when users repeated the quickstart and reproduced the failed state.

claimJudgments.claimText: Pilot interviewees trust recommendations only when each claim links back to inspectable source snippets; they prefer evidence separated by origin (analytics vs support vs interviews) over blended rationales.
claimJudgments.explanation: S3 directly says senior engineers trust recommendations only when claims point back to inspectable source snippets, and that the strongest trust signal was separating analytics, support, and interview evidence rather than blending them.

recommendationJudgment.explanation: The recommendation addresses the requested adoption risks and next product-quality improvement. Prioritizing self-serve setup recovery is strongly supported by S1, S2, S4, S5, and S6 as the main onboarding failure and pilot scheduling gate. The secondary recommendation to implement trace-backed recommendations with citations and evidence-origin labels is supported by S3, S4, and S6. Some implementation details such as explicit state diagnostics are not stated verbatim, but they are reasonable product recommendations grounded in the documented setup/retry failure mode.

citationSupport.note: S1 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S2 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S3 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.

notes: Hybrid evaluator output: deterministic hard checks plus structured LLM judge evidence. Manual spot checks are still required before claiming product improvement.
